records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Recommended; p. pr. & vb. n. Recommending.]

defs: To commend to the favorable notice of another; to commit to another's care, confidence, or acceptance, with favoring representations; to put in a favorable light before any one; to bestow commendation on; as, he recommended resting the mind and exercising the body.
quotes: Maecenas recommended Virgil and Horace to Augustus, whose praises . . . have made him precious to posterity.

defs: To make acceptable; to attract favor to.
quotes: A decent boldness ever meets with friends, Succeeds, and e'en a stranger recommends.

defs: To commit; to give in charge; to commend.
quotes: Paul chose Silas and departed, being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of God.
